package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for goroutine_derived_context_unmanaged: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Likely long-lived goroutines launched after a derived context is created and before the matching cancel call is observed.
type CaseGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanagedDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanagedModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanagedRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanagedModel
}

type CaseGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanagedService struct {
    repo *CaseGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanagedRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanagedAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanaged(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseGoroutineDerivedContextUnmanagedDTO) error {
    go func() {
        http.Get(input)
    }()
    _ = db.Where("id = ?", input).Find(&items)
    return nil
}
